title: Osteospermum hyoseroides
intro: Osteospermum hyoseroides, also known as the blue-eyed daisy, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Osteospermum hyoseroides include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Osteospermum hyoseroides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern and southern parts of South Africa. The classification of Osteospermum hyoseroides includes it being a member of the genus Osteospermum, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as African daisies. This genus is further classified within the tribe Calenduleae, which includes other flowering plants such as the pot marigold and the African daisy. The classification of Osteospermum hyoseroides is rounded out by its classification as a member of the order Asterales.
